sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.86 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Is the group$G = a, b [[a,b],b]=1$, which is isomorphic to the group of all unitriangular automorphisms of the free group of rank 3, linear? { V.\,A.\,Roman'kov} Yes, it is linear, since it embeds in the holomorph${ Hol}\,F_2$of the free group$F_2$, which can be seen by adding a new generator$c=[a,b]$, so that$G = a, b, c a^b=ac,\; c^b=c$, while${ Hol}\,F_2$was shown to be linear in Corollary~3 in...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
